text: The hippopotamus is a large semiaquatic mammal native to sub-Saharan Africa. Despite its stocky shape and short legs, it can outrun a human over short distances.
Hippos spend most of the day in rivers and lakes to keep their skin moist and protected from the sun. At night they leave the water to graze on grasses.
The hippocampus is a brain structure critical for forming new memories and spatial navigation. Damage to the hippocampus can impair the ability to remember recent events.
HippoRAG is a retrieval framework that builds a knowledge graph from passages using open information extraction, then uses personalized PageRank for multi-hop retrieval.
OpenRouter provides a unified API compatible with the OpenAI SDK, allowing access to many language and embedding models through a single API key.
Personalized PageRank seeds the random walk from query-linked entities and phrases, propagating relevance scores across the passage–entity graph.
Dense passage retrieval computes cosine similarity between query and passage embeddings as a fallback when no facts match the query.
